me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.title: Effects of Exercise Training on Patients With Long COVID-19
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeDescription: The cardiopulmonary fitness assessed by Cardiopulmonary Exercise Test

me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.title: Exercise and Post-COVID/ Long-COVID: Effects of Different Training Modalities on Various Parameters in People Affected by the Sequelae of COVID-19
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeMeasure: Change of peak oxygen consumption (VO2peak measured in ml/min/kg)
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeDescription: VO2peak will be assessed during cardio pulmonary exercise testing (CPET) on a bicycle ergometer.

me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.title: Multimodal Long Covid19
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeMeasure: Number of participants with treatment-related adverse events assessed by Cardiorespiratory Capacity
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeDescription: The primary outcome of the study will be measured by a maximal cardiopulmonary exercise test (CPET) on a cycloergometer. The protocol will be individualised and will include a 3-minute warm-up at 20 Watts, followed by an increasing workload of 10-20 W/min (depending on the participant's fitness level) until exhaustion, maintaining a cadence greater than 60 rpm. Electrocardiogram and oxygen saturation will be continuously monitored, while rating of perceived exertion and blood pressure will be measured every two minutes during the test. Continuously measured at rest, as well as during exercise and recovery: minute ventilation, oxygen consumption and carbon dioxide production. Gas exchange shall be collected on a breath-by-breath basis and expressed as a 15-second time average for analysis.

me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.title: Effects of IMT on Functional Capacity in Patients With Chronic COVID After Hospital Discharge
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeDescription: Maximal functional capacity will be evaluated with incremental and symptom-limited cardiopulmonary exercise testing. Peak oxygen consumption (peakVO2) will be considered the highest value of VO2 during the last 20 seconds of exercise.

me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.title: A Study to Systematically Assess the Efficacy and Safety of Intravenous Albumin Infusions in Severe POTS
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeMeasure: Change in maximal exercise capacity - Cardiopulmonary exercise testing
me-cfs:2-day-cpet-vo-peak-day-2.outcomeDescription:
Cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPX) using breath by breath gas-analysis measures variables related to cardiorespiratory function, including expiratory ventilation and pulmonary gas exchange (oxygen uptake (VO2) and carbon dioxide (VCO2).The standard expression of aerobic working capacity is the maximum VO2.

VO2 max reached during a symptom-limited incremental CPX protocol is commonly expressed as O2 per kg-1 per min -1.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Investigating the Effects of Intermittent Hypoxia-Hyperoxia Treatment (IHHT) in People With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS) to Improve Fatigue, Pain, and Quality of Life by Targeting Mitochondrial Dysfunction and Autonomic Nervous System Impairment
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Assessment of functional capacity using the FUNCAP-27 questionnaire.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Evaluating the Impact of a Functional and Cognitive Strategy in Patients With Long Covid-19
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Composite Change in Functional Capacity, Cognitive Function, Health-Related Quality of Life, and Strength
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription:
The primary outcome is a composite measure evaluating the aggregated change across four key functional and cognitive scales:

* Functional Capacity with six-minute walk test.
* Cognitive Function with Montreal Cognitive Assessment, MoCA. The minimum score is 0 points, which would indicate very severe cognitive impairment, since the maximum total score is 30 points. Normally, a score of 26 or higher is considered within the normal range, although the range may vary depending on factors such as the patient's age and educational level. A score below 26 could suggest possible cognitive impairment, and the lower the score, the more indicative it may be of severe cognitive problems.
* Health-Related Quality of Life with EuroQol 5 Dimensions 5 Levels, EQ-5D-5L. Maximum value: 1 This value corresponds to the best possible health state. Minimum value: -0.594 This value corresponds to the worst possible.
* Handgrip Strength with hydraulic dynamometer.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Long Covid (LC)-REVITALIZE - A Long Covid Repurposed Drug Study
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: General participant reported severity of post-exertional malaise (PEM) using the FUNCAP27 questionnaire
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: To compare symptom burden of participants with Long Covid treated with study drug versus placebo by measuring the change in scores of the Functional Capacity (FUNCAP)27 questionnaire from baseline to the interim and final analyses. The FUNCAP27 questionnaire consists of 27 items related to assessing functional capacity and the consequences of performing activities. Each item is scored on a scale of 0-6, with 0 indicating the participant is unable to complete the activity and 6 indicating the activity is unproblematic and does not affect other activities.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Plitidepsin in Adults With Post-COVID-19 Condition (PCC)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: To compare functional capacity changes in patients from each group using the post-COVID-19 Functional State (PCFS) scale.
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Difference between groups in functional capacity on the PCFS scale on day 10 (±2), day 30 (±2), and day 90 (±5) of the follow-up period.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Ensitrelvir for Viral Persistence and Inflammation in People Experiencing Long COVID
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with Ensitrelvir versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean DASI at Day 10 post-randomization. The Duke Activity Status Index is a patient-reported estimate of functional capacity, maximal oxygen consumption (VO2 max) and maximum metabolic equivalent of tasks (METs). The DASI questionnaire produces a score between 0 and 58.2 points, which is linearly correlated with a patient's VO2 max and METs, as measured from cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPET). It inquires about a person's ability to perform self-care, walk, climb stairs, run, do house and yard work, engage in sexual intercourse, and perform moderate recreational activities. A higher score indicates higher functional capacity.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Is Swimming a More Tolerable Form of Movement for Individuals With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/ Chronic Fatigue Syndrome?
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The functional capacity 27 scale will be used to assess the functional severity of each participant's condition. The scale has 27 statements, that are ranked from on a likert scale of 0-6, with 0 being "unable to perform that activity", and 6 being "unproblematic - does not affect other activities." The average score from all 27 statements is used as the overall score. Only participants with a score ≥3.0 and \<5.8 will be included in the study (representing mild-to-moderate ME/CFS).

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Diaphragmatic Breathing Exercises for Post-COVID-19 Diaphragmatic Dysfunction (DD)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: It is a simple test that requires no specialized equipment or advanced training for physicians and assesses the submaximal level of functional capacity of an individual while walking on a flat, hard surface in a period of 6 min. It is used to assess the response to a medical therapy, and in cardiac/pulmonary rehabilitation.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Vagal Approaches on Long COVID-19
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Monitoring steps will allow us to assess whether SAT and Truvaga Electrical Vagus Nerve Stimulator are associated with an increase in average daily steps, which may indicate enhanced functional capacity or reduced fatigue.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Whole-Body Electrostimulation for Functional Recovery in Post-COVID Syndrome
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Functional capacity will be evaluated using the Six-Minute Walk Test (6MWT), which measures the maximum distance walked in six minutes as an indicator of aerobic capacity and functional exercise tolerance.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: The Long COVID Treatment Trial
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Secondary Outcome: Changes in functional capacity as measured by Functional Capacity (FUNCAP) survey
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The 27-question version of FUNCAP has a minimum score of 0 and a maximum score of 162, with a lower score indicating lower functional capacity.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Physiology of Long COVID-19 and the Impact of Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation on Quality-of-Life and Functional Capacity
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Characterizing the impact of exercise training (cardiac rehabilitation) on functional capacity among patients with Long COVID
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: functional capacity as determined by VO2max

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Anti-SARS-CoV-2 Monoclonal Antibodies for Long COVID (COVID-19)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with AER002 versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean DASI at Day 90 post-infusion. The Duke Activity Status Index is a patient-reported estimate of functional capacity, maximal oxygen consumption (VO2 max) and maximum metabolic equivalent of tasks (METs). The DASI questionnaire produces a score between 0 and 58.2 points, which is linearly correlated with a patient's VO2 max and METs, as measured from cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPET). It inquires about a person's ability to perform self-care, walk, climb stairs, run, do house and yard work, engage in sexual intercourse, and perform moderate recreational activities. A higher score indicates higher functional capacity.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Heat thErapy And mobiLity in COVID-19 Survivors
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Test of physical functional capacity

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: LIFT: Life Improvement Trial
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription:
Change in the total FUNCAP55 questionnaire score

The FUNCAP55 is a questionnaire developed to assess the functional capacity of patients. Each question is answered on a 6-point scale. The greater the score, the better the functioning.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Use of a Cysteine-rich Whey Protein Isolate in Post COVID-19 Cognitive Impairment
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Functional capacity will be measured by the 30-second sit-to-stand test. The number of repetitions completed will be compared between baseline and study completion.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: SARS-CoV-2 Specific Monoclonal Antibody for Post-COVID-19 Conditions (Long COVID)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Change in Heart Rate post 6-minute walk distance (6MWT), in meters, from baseline to Weeks 12 and 24.Unit: Beats per minute (bpm)

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Cognitive Rehabilitation Therapy for COVID-19
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Change in functional capacity performance z score
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Change in composite z score

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation in Post-acute COVID-19 Syndrome
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: CPET is a non-invasive examination, which provides assessment of the integrative exercise responses involving the pulmonary, cardiovascular, hematopoietic, neuropsychological, and skeletal muscle systems. It permits the evaluation of both submaximal and peak exercise responses. CPET is currently act as a clinical applications for the evaluation of undiagnosed exercise intolerance and for the objective determination of functional capacity and impairment.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: CISCO-21 Prevent and Treat Long COVID-19.
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This is a validated measure of functional capacity, with test-retest reliability and evidence of being responsive to rehabilitation interventions.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Pulmonary Rehabilitation for Long COVID (Post COVID-19 Condition)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Predictors of response in 6- minute walk distance (6MWD)
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The predictors will be based on baseline symptom scores (CAT, mMRC, CIS), baseline 6MWD and hospitalisation status.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Mindfulness in Post Acute Sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 Infection (PASC) Dysautonomia
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Change from baseline of Six Minute Walk in hemodynamic parameters, exercise tolerance
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Six Minute Walk. The six-minute walk (6MWT) was developed in 1963 by Balke to evaluate functional capacity and endurance during physical activity. The standardized six-minute walk test (6MWT) measures the maximum distance an individual is able to walk over a total of six minutes. The individual is allowed to self-pace and rest as needed as they traverse back and forth along a flat marked walkway. Baseline heart rate and oxygen saturation are measured then continuously monitored to identify the lowest oxygen saturation, which may occur before the end of the test. The patient's baseline and post-test perceived dyspnea and fatigue are rated using the Borg scale 0-10 (0 none- 10 maximum).

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Effects of Physiotherapy Via Telerehabilitation in Patients With COVID-19
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: using Duke Activity status index The final score ranges between zero and 58.2 points; The higher the score, the better the functional capacity.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: tDCS in the Management of Post-COVID Disorders
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: the participant's functional capacity and aerobic resistance will be evaluated through the 6-minute walk test. The test measures the distance that the volunteer covers in a period of 6 minutes walking at a steady speed.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: The Use of Photobiomodulation in the Treatment of Oral Complaints of Long COVID-19.A Randomized Controlled Trial.
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Brazilian version of the SF 36 Quality of Life Scale
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Assessment of general quality of life, translated and validated for the Brazilian population, composed of assessments in the following domains: functional capacity, limitation due to physical aspects, pain, general health status, vitality, social aspects, emotional aspects and mental health.

me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Telerehabilitation for Post COVID-19 Condition
me-cfs:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Strength, assistance with walking, rising from a chair, climbing stairs, and falls questionnaire (SARC-F)

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: PERCEIVE-Outreach: A Scalable, Risk-Based Model for Managing Persistent Cardiovascular Impact in Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in Assessment of Quality of Life (AQoL) 8D at 12 & 24 months
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Publicly accessible Australian utility score (online); range 0.06 (worse) to 1.00 (better)

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: sElective Serotonin reuPtake inhibitoRs In posT-covid After COVID-19
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Health-related Quality of Life
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Dutch-Flemish Patient-Reported Outcome Measurement Information System (PROMIS) Profile-29. Higher scores indicate better outcome.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of PNMR as Treatment for Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in QoL as measured by the Medical Outcomes Study Short Form 36 (SF-36) at 3 and 6 weeks on treatment.
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The SF-36 (Short Form 36) is a self-reported survey that measures 36 individual health-related indicators of Quality of Life (QOL). It asks the patient to rate their: General Health; General Health now compared to one week ago; along with more detailed evaluations of Physical Health, Role Limitations (Physical), Emotional Health, Role Limitations (Emotional), Social Functioning; and to rate other factors related to Pain, Energy, and Mood as compared to the week prior. Each question has a unique set of answers specific to the question. For some questions a higher score means a worse outcome, whereas for other questions a higher score means a better outcome. This method requires that the patient think through each answer individually.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Diaphragmatic Breathing Exercises With KOTS Currents in Patients With Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Quality of Life with SF-36 questionnaire
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Quality of life will be assessed using the SF-36 questionnaire. The outcome will evaluate the change in quality-of-life dimensions from baseline to after the three-week intervention. Higher scores indicate better quality of life.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: RECOVER-AUTONOMIC: Platform Protocol, Appendix A (IVIG)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS-29) + 2 Questionnaire
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription:
The PROMIS-29 consists of 29 items that assess general domains of health and functioning, including overall physical health, mental health, social health, pain, fatigue, and overall perceived quality of life.

The PROMIS-29+2 is used to calculate a preference score (PROPr) by the addition of two Cognitive Function Ability items. Scores will be reported as T scores ranging from 0 to 100, with a score of 60 being 1 standard deviation above the mean. Higher scores indicate worse overall health.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Evaluating the Effects of a Fermented Diet on Microbiome Diversity in Individuals With Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Assessment of quality of life
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Measured using the self-reported EuroQol-5D-3L questionnaire. Total questions: 6. Mobility: 1, Self-Care: 1, Usual Activities: 1, Pain/discomfort: 1, Anxiety/depression: 1, Health State - Visual Analog Scale: 1. 3L - 3 levels of severity: no problems, some problems, extreme problems. The visual analog scale ranges from 0 to 100 with higher scores reflecting better perceived current health-related quality of life state.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: NE3107 in Adults With Neurological Symptoms of Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change from Baseline in SF-12 Health Survey (Physical and Mental Component Scores)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Generic quality-of-life assessment evaluating physical (PCS) and mental (MCS) health domains

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Diagnostic and Clinical Management of Non-Obstructive Myocardial Ischemia in Patients With Long Covid-19 Syndrome and New Onset Chest Pain
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: The changes in angina symptom and quality of life assessed by Seattle Questionnaire of Angina score in the treatment guided group comparing to the standard of care group
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The Seattle Angina Questionnaire (SAQ) measures 5 dimensions of coronary artery disease (physical limit, stability, frequency, satisfaction, disease perception) on a 0-100 scale, where higher scores indicate better health status. Scores are interpreted as: 0-24 (Poor), 25-49 (Fair), 50-74 (Good), 75-100 (Excellent).

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Taurine Supplementation in Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Health Related Quality of Life - SF-36 (v.1)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Measurement of change in quality of life using the Quality of Life - SF-36 (v.1) assessment tool.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: AT1001 for the Treatment of Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Adverse Event Profiling and Time to Symptom Resolution
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The primary objective of this study is to evaluate the safety and efficacy of Larazotide (AT1001) versus placebo in children and young adults 7 to ≤50 years of age who present with symptoms of Long COVID. Safety will be assessed by means of adverse event monitoring, and efficacy will be evaluated using the Symptom Burden Questionnaire™ for Long COVID (SBQ™-LC) survey. Additional surveys will be used to monitor symptoms, quality of life and limitation of activities before, during and after treatment with Larazotide or Placebo.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Investigating the Effects of Intermittent Hypoxia-Hyperoxia Treatment (IHHT) in People With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS) to Improve Fatigue, Pain, and Quality of Life by Targeting Mitochondrial Dysfunction and Autonomic Nervous System Impairment
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in SF-36 Vitality Domain score from baseline to post-treatment
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Change in the vitality domain of the 36-Item Short Form Health Survey (SF-36), assessing fatigue and energy-related quality of life.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: SARS-CoV-2 and Herpesvirus Inhibition for Ending Long COVID Dysfunction
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: The EuroQol Five-Dimensional Health Questionnaire (EQ-5D-5L)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The EQ-5D-5L is a validated, standardized, generic instrument that is a preference-based health- related quality of life questionnaire. The descriptive system comprises five dimensions: mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort and anxiety/depression. Each dimension has 5 levels: no problems, slight problems, moderate problems, severe problems and extreme problems. The patient is asked to indicate their health state by ticking the box next to the most appropriate statement in each of the five dimensions. This decision results in a 1-digit number that expresses the level selected for that dimension. The digits for the five dimensions can be combined into a 5-digit number that describes the patient's health state. Full scale from 5 to 25, with higher score indicating poorer health outcomes.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Fatigability in Long COVID-19
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Health-related quality of life (SF-36)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The 36-item short-form (SF-36) is a multi-item scale that assesses eight health concepts: 1) limitations in physical activities because of health problems; 2) limitations in social activities because of physical or emotional problems; 3) limitations in usual role activities because of physical health problems; 4) bodily pain; 5) general mental health (psychological distress and well-being); 6) limitations in usual role activities because of emotional problems; 7) vitality (energy and fatigue); and 8) general health perceptions.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Low Dose Sirolimus in People With Post-Acute Sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC) Long COVID-19
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: European Quality of Life-Visual Analogue Scale (EQ-VAS)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The EQ VAS records the patient's self-rated health on a visual analogue scale where the endpoints are labelled 'The best health you can imagine' (100) and 'The worst health you can imagine' (0), with higher scores indicating better health state. The VAS can be used as a quantitative measure of health outcome that reflects the patient's own judgement.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: A Study to Assess Anktiva in Patients With Long Covid-19.
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in other assessments (eg, EuroQoL Quality of Life) from baseline, intervention 2, FU2.3 (2 weeks after last NAI administration), FU2.5, and EOS.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Trial EValuating Baricitinib on PERSistent NEurologic and Cardiopulmonary Symptoms of Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Quality-of-life measures including the EuroQOL-5D-5L at 3-, 6-, and 12-months
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The 5-Level EuroQol-5D health questionnaire (EQ-5D-5L) is a standardized instrument for use as a measure of health outcome that includes a descriptive system consisting of 5 dimensions (mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort, and anxiety/depression). The index value total (which is country-specific) ranges from a minimum value of -1 to a maximum value of +1. A higher EQ-5D-5L index value represents better quality of life (QoL), thus a positive change in the index value represents improved QoL.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: RCT of Mind-body in Long COVID and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Physical functioning
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Physical functioning of health-related quality of life (HRQL) as measured by the Short Form 36-item Health Survey (SF-36) (V1)

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: RECOVER-ENERGIZE Platform Protocol_Appendix B (Structured Pacing (PEM))
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in quality of life, as measured by the PROMIS-29+2
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The PROMIS-29+2 is used to calculate a preference score (PROPr) by the addition of two Cognitive Function Ability items. Preference-based scores provide an overall summary of health-related quality of life on a common metric. Preference-based scores summarize multiple domains on a metric ranging from 0 (as bad as dead) to 1 (perfect or ideal health).

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation as a Complementary Therapy to Exercise in Chronic Fatigue
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Medical Outcome Study Short Form questionnaire (MOS-SF 12)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Quality of life assessed by the MOS-SF 12 questionnaire , score from 0 to 100. A low score reflects a perception of poor health, loss of function and the presence of pain. A high score reflects a perception of good health, absence of functional deficit and pain.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Cognitive Muscular Therapy for Patients With Long-COVID and Breathing Pattern Disorder
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Change in EQ-5D-5L
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Used to capture an individual's quality of life through their ability to complete daily living activities. Each heading is rated from no problems through to unable to complete the task.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Digital Multimodal Rehabilitation for People With Post-acute COVID-19 Syndrome.
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Differences between groups in scores of Quality of Life
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Quality of Life is measured with EuroQol a self-completion questionnaire, which consists of five questions: covering mobility, hygiene, activities, pain, and anxiety. The descriptive system divides each of the 5 dimensions into three levels of response: the absence of a problem, some problem, and extreme problem. Lower scores indicate better outcomes. In addition, the questionnaire has a plus scale where the participants rated their health state on a scale of 0-100. In this scale, higher scores indicate better outcome.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Pycnogenol® in Post-COVID-19 Condition
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Health-related quality of life (EQ-5D-5L)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: The EQ-5D-5L assesses the 5 dimensions mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort, anxiety/depression. The EQ-5D-5L scores each dimension on five levels of severity ranging from 1 = "no problems" to 5 = "extreme problems. The instrument will be used at baseline and after 12 weeks.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Tele-exercise Training Program for Long COVID
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Health-Related Quality of Life
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription:
The brief World Health Organization Quality of Life questionnaire includes 26 standardized items covering four domains: physical, psychological, social, and environmental. It also contains two general items assessing overall quality of life and general health. The Taiwanese version adds two culturally specific items related to social respect/acceptance and eating/food.

All items are rated on a 5-point Likert scale, where higher scores reflect better quality of life. Scores for each domain are calculated by averaging the item scores within that domain and then multiplying by 4, yielding a final score range from 4 to 20.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Evaluating the Impact of a Functional and Cognitive Strategy in Patients With Long Covid-19
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: Composite Change in Functional Capacity, Cognitive Function, Health-Related Quality of Life, and Strength
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription:
The primary outcome is a composite measure evaluating the aggregated change across four key functional and cognitive scales:

* Functional Capacity with six-minute walk test.
* Cognitive Function with Montreal Cognitive Assessment, MoCA. The minimum score is 0 points, which would indicate very severe cognitive impairment, since the maximum total score is 30 points. Normally, a score of 26 or higher is considered within the normal range, although the range may vary depending on factors such as the patient's age and educational level. A score below 26 could suggest possible cognitive impairment, and the lower the score, the more indicative it may be of severe cognitive problems.
* Health-Related Quality of Life with EuroQol 5 Dimensions 5 Levels, EQ-5D-5L. Maximum value: 1 This value corresponds to the best possible health state. Minimum value: -0.594 This value corresponds to the worst possible.
* Handgrip Strength with hydraulic dynamometer.

me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.title: Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Plitidepsin in Adults With Post-COVID-19 Condition (PCC)
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeMeasure: To compare changes in terms of Quality of Life (QoL) for each group.
me-cfs:sf-36-physical-function-score.outcomeDescription: Difference between groups according to the EuroQoL-5D questionnaire on day 10 (±2), day 30 (±2), and day 90 (±5) of the follow-up period.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Phase I Open-Label Safety Trial of Pembrolizumab for Neurological Post- Acute Sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 (PD1-PASC I)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: The primary outcome of this study is to determine the safety of a single dose of intravenous Pembrolizumab in participants with neurological post-acute sequalae of SARS-CoV-2 infection.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: A Multi-Center, Individually-Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Group, Superiority Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy of the Combination of Maraviroc and Atorvastatin for the Treatment of Subjects With Long COVID
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Fatigue
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Fatigue will be measured via PROMIS Fatigue v1.0. The PROMIS Fatigue 10a assesses fatigue severity in the previous week. Fatigue is divided into the experience of fatigue (frequency, duration, and intensity) and the impact of fatigue on physical, mental, and social activities. The instrument translates the total raw score into a T-score with a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 10. A score of 50 is the average for the United States general population (SD=10).

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of PNMR as Treatment for Long COVID
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Change in distance walked on the 6-minute walk test (6MWT) after 6 weeks of treatment.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Patient baseline is first assessed at Day 0 upon screening, and then retested after 6 weeks of treatment.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: A Study to Examine Anktiva for the Treatment of COVID-19.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Percent increase or decrease change in the Absolute lymphocyte count (as measured on CBC) from Screening to End Of Study with various timepoints.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: A Study to Assess Anktiva in Patients With Long Covid-19.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Change in the ALC from Screening, INT1, FU1.3, INT2, FU2.3, FU2.4, FU2.5, and EOS.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: RECOVER-ENERGIZE Platform Protocol_Appendix B (Structured Pacing (PEM))
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Change in orthostatic hypotension, as measured by the Modified Orthostatic Hypotension Questionnaire (mOHQ)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The Orthostatic Hypotension Questionnaire (OHQ) is a measure of orthostatic intolerance. The modified OHQ (mOHQ) measure used in this study includes a total of ten items related to daily activities and symptoms.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Digital Multimodal Rehabilitation for People With Post-acute COVID-19 Syndrome.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Differences between groups in scores of global cognition
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Global cognition was assessed with the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) a screening tool designed to identify mild cognitive impairment (MCI) and other cognitive deficits. The MoCA takes around 10-15 minutes to complete and consists of 30 items (range=0-30). Higher scores mean a better outcome.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Pycnogenol® in Post-COVID-19 Condition
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Functional exercise capacity
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: A 30 second Sit-to-Stand (STS) will be performed at baseline and after 12 weeks. The number of repetitions that the participant completes the full sit-to-stand movement on a chair during 30 seconds. A familiarisation test will be done at the screening visit to rule out potential learning effects.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Harnessing Optimism and Perseverance in the Face of Long COVID-Español
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Patient Health Questionnaire-8 Item (PHQ-8)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Patient health questionnaire-8 (PHQ-8) is a multipurpose instrument for screening, diagnosing, monitoring and measuring the severity of depression. PHQ-8 is an 8-item instrument. Each question is rated on a scale of 0 to 3, total score scale from 0 - 24. Lower scores indicate minimal depression (better outcome) and higher scores indicate severe depression (worse outcome).

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Lumbrokinase for Adults With Long Covid, Post-treatment Lyme Disease Syndrome, and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-2)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: A PHQ-2 score ranges from 0 to 6, with 3 being the optimal cut-off point for depression screening. A score of 3 or higher indicates a high probability of major depressive disorder. A score of 2 or higher may be preferred in situations where clinicians want to ensure that few cases of depression are missed.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Antiviral Clinical Trial for Long Covid-19
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: EuroQol 5-Dimension 5-Level (EQ-5D-5L) Visual Analogue Scale
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The EQ-5D-5L is a standardized measure of health status that consists of 5 dimensions (mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort, anxiety/depression) each with 5 levels of severity, and a visual analogue scale (VAS) where individuals rate their overall health from 0 (worst imaginable health) to 100 (best imaginable health).

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: RECLAIM: Recovering From COVID-19 Lingering Symptoms Adaptive Integrative Medicine
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Mental Health - General Anxiety Assessment Form (GAD-7)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The GAD-7 is a valid and efficient tool for screening for generalized anxiety disorder and assessing its severity in clinical practice and research. It is an easy-to-use, self-administered patient questionnaire that can be completed in minutes.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Recovering From COVID-19 Lingering Symptoms Adaptive Integrative Medicine Trial - Effect of Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy for the Treatment of Post COVID Condition
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Mental Health - General Anxiety Assessment Form (GAD-7)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The GAD-7 is a valid and efficient tool for screening for generalized anxiety disorder and assessing its severity in clinical practice and research. It is an easy-to-use, self-administered patient questionnaire that can be completed in minutes.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: An Open-label, Clinical Feasibility Study of the Efficacy of Remdesivir for Long-COVID.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: To assess the feasibility of the use of Remdesivir in the treatment of patients with Long COVID.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: To ascertain screening and recruitment rates (overall and by different recruitment pathways).

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: A Trial of Shengmai Liquid for Long COVID Fatigue.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Traditional Chinese Medicine Syndrome Score Scale
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Measured once during the screening period before the start of the trial, and then at 30 days and 60 days during the trial, for a total of three measurements.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Study to Investigate Improvement in Physical Function in SF-36 With Vericiguat Compared With Placebo in Participants With Post-COVID-19 Syndrome
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Improvement in cognitive function measured by the Symbol Digit Modalities Test (SDMT)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The Symbol Digit Modalities Test (SDMT) is a screening instrument commonly used to assess neurological dysfunction. It detects cognitive impairment as well as changes in cognitive functioning over time and in response to treatment. The scores range between 0 and 110 (higher scores equate to greater cognitive functioning). Intra-patient change in cognitive score from baseline to week ten.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: The Effect of Acupuncture Treatment on Cognitive Functions and Brain Networks for Long COVID
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Change of Addenbrooke's Cognitive Examination-III total score from baseline to the end of 8 weeks
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Addenbrooke's Cognitive Examination-III is a cognitive screening tool, and the total score assess general cognitive function. The total score ranges from 0 to 100. Higher score indicates better general cognitive function

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Immunoadsorption in Patients With Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Including Patients With Post-COVID-19 CFS
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Improvement in neurocognitive functions as measured by the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: MoCA is a sensitive and validated cognitive screening tool to test subjects quickly and accurately for mild cognitive impairment, irrespective of etiology. A person can gain a maximum of 30 points, and professionals consider a score of 26 or above to be normal. A score of 25 points or less may indicate some degree of cognitive impairment (18-25 = mild cognitive impairment, 10-17 = moderate cognitive impairment, fewer than 10 points = severe cognitive impairment). Intra-patient change in neurocognitive functions at month 3 and at month 6 (if required by the investigator) after IA completion compared to baseline.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Vagal Approaches on Long COVID-19
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Patient Health Questionnaire-8 (PHQ-8)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The PHQ-8 is a gold-standard screening tool for major depressive disorder, endorsed by the CDC and WHO, with strong criterion validity and sensitivity to clinical change.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Immunoadsorption in Autoimmune Long COVID
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Autoantibody score using an in-house Luminex assay
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: For this assay, IgG will be isolated from patient serum samples obtained during screening and analyzed using a Luminex-based multiplex immunoassay targeting a panel of 15 validated autoantigens. A weighted scoring system is applied to the resulting autoantibody signals.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Design and Validation of Innovative Strategies Based on Dual-Task Approach
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Montreal Cognitive Assessment
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The Montreal Cognitive Assessment is a widely used screening tool designed to evaluate global cognitive function. It assesses multiple domains, including attention, executive functions, memory, language, visuospatial abilities, abstraction, and orientation. The test is quick to administer, sensitive to mild cognitive impairment, and commonly applied in both clinical practice and research settings. The maximum score is 30, 25 is a cut off for normal cognitive functioning, with higher score denoting better performance.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Sauna for Long Covid
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: a well-established, brief self-administered 9-item depression screening and monitoring tool. Uses a range of 0-27 with higher scores indicating greater depressive symptoms.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Stellate Ganglion Block for COVID-induced Parosmia
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Participants will be asked to complete the HADS, which screens for both anxiety and depression in the general population. It consists of 7 questions for anxiety and 7 questions for depression each ranked on a 4-point Likert Scale. A score of 0-7 is considered normal, 8-10 is borderline abnormal anxiety or depression, and a score of 11-21 corresponds with screening positive for anxiety or depression.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Effect of Hi-OxSR for the Treatment of Post COVID Condition
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Mental Health - General Anxiety Assessment Form (GAD-7)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: The GAD-7 is a valid and efficient tool for screening for generalized anxiety disorder and assessing its severity in clinical practice and research. It is an easy-to-use, self-administered patient questionnaire that can be completed in minutes.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.title: Antiviral Strategies in the Prevention of Long-term Cardiovascular Outcomes Following COVID-19: The paxloviD/Remdesivir Effectiveness For the prEvention of loNg coviD Clinical Trial
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeMeasure: Recruitment rate
lyme:b-burgdorferi-eia-screening.outcomeDescription: Recruitment rate, defined as the proportion of eligible people who consent and are randomized into the pilot trial, which will inform the feasibility of a full scale trial.

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: Standard blood labs include C-Reactive Protein, Interleukin-6, metabolic panel, and lipid panels

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.title: Effects of Immulina TM Supplements With PASC Patients

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: Measures the change in interleukin-6 (IL-6) levels

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: IL-6 will be assessed via blood sample.

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.title: Effects of Cacao Flavonoids in Long COVID-19 Patients (FLALOC)
Pilot Study of Vitamin K2 (MK-7) and Vitamin D3 Supplementation and the Effects on PASC Symptomatology and Inflammatory Biomarkers
lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeMeasure: Change in interleukin 6 (IL-6) as measured by blood test

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.title: Exercise for Health in Patients With Post-acute Sequelae of COVID-19
lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: Interleukin 6 and 10 (IL-6 and IL-10) in ng/mL

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.title: A Practical RCT of TCM in the Treatment of LCOVID and Analysis of Syndrome Types and Medication Characteristics.
lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeMeasure: Serum level change of inflammatory factors
lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: Inflammatory markers including interferon (IFN-β), interferon (IFN-λ2/3), pentraxin 3 (PTX3) and interleukin 6 will be measured.

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeMeasure: Change of interleukin-6 (IL-6 measured in pg/ml)
lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeDescription: IL-6 will be assessed via blood sample.

lyme:interleukin-6-il-6.outcomeMeasure: Change in the concentration of interleukin-6 and interleukin 1b

lyme:interferon-ifn.outcomeDescription: Difference in Interferon gamma from baseline to 20 weeks.

lyme:interferon-ifn.outcomeDescription: Change in blood cytokine profile will be assessed, including levels of interferon gamma, interleukins, leptin, vascular endothelial growth factor, membrane-bound immunoglobulin, and tumour necrosis factor-α etc.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Standard blood labs include C-Reactive Protein, Interleukin-6, metabolic panel, and lipid panels

Complete Blood Count (CBC) including biomarkers of inflammation Physical Examination including electrocardiogram (EKG)

Change in laboratory values including inflammatory markers, e.g., C-reactive protein (CRP), erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR), Antinuclear Antibody (ANA)

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Acupuncture and Chinese Herbal Medicine for Long COVID
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Change in Laboratory Biomarkers - C-reactive protein
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Laboratory values will be compared from baseline to Week 8 to assess physiological effects of the intervention. Test includes C-reactive protein (CRP). Changes in this marker will indicate biological trends in inflammation.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Anti-SARS-CoV-2 Monoclonal Antibodies for Long COVID (COVID-19)
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with AER002 versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean CRP concentration (mg/L) at Day 90 post-infusion.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Differences in C-Reactive Protein from baseline to 12 weeks.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Long COVID-19 Syndrome Lifestyle Intervention Study
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Improvement of long-COVID syndrome signs: Measures of inflammation

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Effect of Metabolic Modulation on a Post-acute COVID-19 Vaccination Syndrome (PACVS) Cohort
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription:
C-reactive protein (CRP) , a marker of inflammation, has been found to be significantly different between long covid groups vs healthy individuals, as well as between long covid and recovered individuals.

CRP is quantitatively measured at a laboratory attached to the study clinic using the Roche Cobas b 101 Blood Analyser using Roche cobas b 101 CRP Test Discs, supplied in packs of 10. Each test required a 12 μL sample of capillary whole blood, obtained via finger prick.

Upon completion of the assay, CRP concentrations are recorded in mg/L with that patient's identification number. The analytical range is 3.0-400 mg/L; if a test returns an error, the value will be reported as missing.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Evaluating Immunomodulatory Interventions in Post-Acute Sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 InfEction
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Measures the change in high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hsCRP) levels

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Plasma levels of C-reactive protein, high-sensitive assay (hsCRP). Higher levels indicate more inflammation

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation in Long COVID-19 Patients With Persistent Breathlessness and Fatigue
BIO 300 Oral Suspension in Previously Hospitalized Long COVID Patients
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Monitoring of blood serum levels for bilirubin, C-reactive protein (CRP), creatinine, blood urea nitrogen (BUN), cholesterol and triglycerides (all reported as mg/dL)

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Pilot Study of Vitamin K2 (MK-7) and Vitamin D3 Supplementation and the Effects on PASC Symptomatology and Inflammatory Biomarkers
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Change in high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hs-CRP) as measured by blood test

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Factors of inflamatory and prothrombotic processes: D-Dimer, N-terminal prohormone of brain natriuretic peptide (NT Pro-BNP), C-reactive protenin and Antinuclear antibodies (ANA)
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: To analyze wether the factors of inflamatory and prothrombotic processes (D-Dimer, Pro-BNP, C-reactive protein, and ANA) at the begining of the study are response predictors to the treatment.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Exercise Interventions in Post-acute Sequelae of Covid-19
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: C-reactive protein (mg/dl) will be used as a biomarker for inflammation to be measured pre- and post-intervention

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Evaluation of C-Reactive Protein, InterLeukin-6 and D-dimer progression during the clinical course of SARS-CoV-2 infection in outpatients

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Change of high-sensitive C-reactive protein (hs-CRP measured in mg/l)
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: hs-CRP will be assessed via blood sample.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: COVID-19 Sequelae: Treatment and Monitoring. A Dietary Supplement Based on Sea Urchin Eggs With Echinochroma A
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Change of Von Willebrand Factor over time-points (baseline, 4 weeks, 12 weeks, 12 weeks) via blood sample.
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Von Willebrand factor will be measured by an automated, highly sensitive and specific immuno-turbidimetric assay (Liatest antigenic Stago, France. VWF in %. VWF an "acute phase protein" which increases in infections (bacterial, less viral and fungi), inflammation, post-OP, malignant processes, and represent the best biomarkers as endothelial pertubation.

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Complementary Treatment of PG2 to Improve Clinical Benefit Response and Quality of Life in Fatigue
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: The blood c-reactive protein level which is related to weight change

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: CRP (C-reactive protein) will be measured at baseline and the eight-week visit (The Measurement of Biomarker Response)

lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.title: Effect of CoQ10 Plus Selenium Supplementation on Clinical Outcomes and Biochemical Markers in ME/CFS (CoSeME Study)
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeMeasure: Measurement of biomarkers of inflammatory immune response.
lyme:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription: Change of the circulating biomarker levels of inflammatory cytokines (IL-1 beta, IL-6, IL-8, IL-10, TNF-alpha, and C-reactive protein) from baseline will be measured.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Investigating the Effects of Intermittent Hypoxia-Hyperoxia Treatment (IHHT) in People With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS) to Improve Fatigue, Pain, and Quality of Life by Targeting Mitochondrial Dysfunction and Autonomic Nervous System Impairment
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Objective autonomic, neurophysiological, and functional performance measures
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Objective assessments of autonomic function, sudomotor function, neurophysiology, tissue oxygenation, cognitive performance, and physical performance.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Multiparametric [18F]F-AraG Imaging in Post-Acute Sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Quantification of [¹⁸F]F-AraG uptake kinetics in PASC and control participants
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: To assess immune activation in convalescent COVID-19, dynamic PET/CT scans will be analyzed to generate time-activity curves (TACs) in multiple tissues. Kinetic modeling will be applied to extract uptake parameters including SUV, SUVR, Vt, Ki, and k3. These values will be reported and statistically compared between PASC and control participants.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Obesity, Insulin Resistance, and PASC: Persistent SARS-CoV-2
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Concentration of Viral RNA in Adipose Tissue
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Investigate the expression of viral RNA in adipose tissue in response to COVID-19 infection. Compare adipose tissue transcripts such as known MODY transcription factors measured by PCR between COVID-19 infected participants and healthy controls.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Fascial Tissue Response to Manual Therapy: Implications in Long COVID-19
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Will be evaluated by MyotonPro (MytonPro, Myoton Ltd.s., Tartu, Estonia)

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Stem Cell Study for Long COVID-19 Neurological Symptoms
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Determine the safety and establish the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) of allogenic human cord tissue derived MSCs (hCTMSCs) as determined by infusional toxicity of the cell product.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Chemoreflex and Baroreflex Alterations Causing Postural Tachycardia Syndrome With Orthostatic Hyperpnea and Hypocapnia
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Orthostatic Changes in Segmental Blood Flow
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Segmental Blood Flows (ml•min-1•100 ml tissue-1) delimit the orthostatic response. Two separate orthostatic tests are used: a standing test and a 70 degree upright tilt test. The standing test will delineate the carotid blood flow signal that sensitizes the carotid body chemoreflex. The tilt test will delineate the effects of sustained tachyardia (and hyperpnea) on systemic hemodynamics and breathing.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Is Post-exertional Symptom Exacerbation Specific to Patients With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis / Chronic Fatigue Syndrome? A Study Comparing Patients With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis / Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and Patients With Cardiac Diseases Who Underwent an Exercise Test.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeMeasure: Variations in oxyhemoglobin and deoxyhemoglobin concentrations measured by Near Infrared Spectroscopy (NIRS) at the muscular level before, during and after exercise.
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Measurement of changes in oxyhaemoglobin and deoxyhaemoglobin concentrations in muscle tissue using Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (NIRS).

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy for Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS)
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription:
Body composition will be assessed using the seca mBCA Ultra, a clinically validated segmental multi-frequency bioelectrical impedance analysis (SMF-BIA) system.

The device estimates fat mass, fat-free mass, skeletal muscle mass, and total body water based on multi-frequency impedance measurements across body segments.

Outcomes will include absolute values (kg or liters) and relative values (percentage of body weight) for each compartment. Higher fat mass and body fat percentage indicate greater adiposity, whereas higher fat-free mass and skeletal muscle mass indicate greater lean tissue.

lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.title: Yan Nian Jiu Zhuan Fa Improve Chronic Fatigue Syndromes
lyme:b-burgdorferi-pcr-blood-csf-tissue.outcomeDescription: Substance P is a bioactive 11-amino acid peptide (Arg-Pro-Lys-Pro-Gln-Gln-Phe-Phe-Gly-Leu-Met-amide) first isolated in 1931 from brain and intestine. The peptide is involved in many physiological processes including pain modulation, smooth muscle contraction, blood pressure control, kidney function and water homeostasis.Substance P is widely distributed in numerous tissues and body fluids including the central and peripheral nervous system, gastrointestinal tract, respiratory tract, visual system and circulatory system.

lyme:esr-erythrocyte-sedimentation-rate.title: Humoral and Cellular Immune Responses After Influenza Vaccination in Patients With Postcancer Fatigue and in Patients With Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
lyme:esr-erythrocyte-sedimentation-rate.outcomeMeasure: Humoral and cellular immune responses after influenza vaccination in patients with postcancer fatigue and in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
lyme:esr-erythrocyte-sedimentation-rate.outcomeDescription: Humoral and cellular immune responses after influenza vaccination in patients with postcancer fatigue and in patients with chronic fatigue syndrome. The humoral immune responses will be measured by the hemagglutination-inhibition antibody test. The cellular immune responses will be measured by T lymphocyte proliferation and cytokine secretion of peripheral blood mononuclear cells. A full blood cell count will be performed and hemoglobin, glucose, and cholesterol levels, iron status, electrolyte balance, erythrocyte sedimentation rate, and thyroid, kidney, and liver function will be checked.

gulf-war-illness:c-reactive-protein-crp.outcomeDescription:
C-reactive protein (CRP) , a marker of inflammation, has been found to be significantly different between long covid groups vs healthy individuals, as well as between long covid and recovered individuals.

CRP is quantitatively measured at a laboratory attached to the study clinic using the Roche Cobas b 101 Blood Analyser using Roche cobas b 101 CRP Test Discs, supplied in packs of 10. Each test required a 12 μL sample of capillary whole blood, obtained via finger prick.

Upon completion of the assay, CRP concentrations are recorded in mg/L with that patient's identification number. The analytical range is 3.0-400 mg/L; if a test returns an error, the value will be reported as missing.

gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.title: Effect of Telerehabilitation Practice in Long COVID-19 Patients
gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.outcomeMeasure: Change from baseline the mean Glutathione (GSH)/ oxidized GSH (GSSG) ratio at 12 weeks
gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.outcomeDescription: The ratio of reduced Glutathione (GSH) to oxidized GSH (GSSG) is an indicator of cellular health, with reduced GSH constituting up to 98% of cellular GSH under normal conditions. It will be measured using The Glutathione GSH/GSSG Assay Kit, which is designed to accurately measure total, reduced and oxidized glutathione in biological samples using an enzymatic method that utilizes Ellman's Reagent (DTNB) and glutathione reductase (GR).

gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.title: Peripheral Dopamine in Postural Tachycardia Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.outcomeMeasure: Plasma Catecholamines (Norepinephrine) After the Last Dose of Placebo or Carbidopa
gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.outcomeDescription: Blood samples were collected while resting supine for at least 30 minutes and 2 to 4 hours after lunch. For catecholamine measurements, blood was collected in chilled vacuum tubes with EDTA. Plasma was separated and stored with added reduced glutathione (Amersham International PLC) at -70°C until the assay. Plasma catecholamines were measured by a method that involves batch alumina extraction followed by high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) for separation with electrochemical detection and quantification.

gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.title: Assessment of N-Acetylcysteine as Therapy for Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome

gulf-war-illness:glutathione-gsh.outcomeDescription: The level of GSH will be represented as the GSH to total creatine (Cr) ratio which is measured by Proton Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy(MRS)

gulf-war-illness:gsh-gssg-ratio.outcomeDescription: The ratio of reduced Glutathione (GSH) to oxidized GSH (GSSG) is an indicator of cellular health, with reduced GSH constituting up to 98% of cellular GSH under normal conditions. It will be measured using The Glutathione GSH/GSSG Assay Kit, which is designed to accurately measure total, reduced and oxidized glutathione in biological samples using an enzymatic method that utilizes Ellman's Reagent (DTNB) and glutathione reductase (GR).

gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.title: Anti-SARS-CoV-2 Monoclonal Antibodies for Long COVID (COVID-19)
gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with AER002 versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean COMPASS-31 score at Day 90 post-infusion. COMPASS-31 asks 31 questions related to autonomic dysfunction. The answer to each question generates a numeric score for the question, which is then summed at the end of the questionnaire. A total score out of 100 is generated summarizing orthostatic intolerance, vasomotor, secretomotor, gastrointestinal, urinary, pupillomotor, temperature intolerance, and sexual impairment. The total score ranges from 0 to 100 and a higher score indicates more severe autonomic dysfunction.

gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.title: Double-Blind Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial of a Proprietary Full Hemp Flower Formulation for Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.outcomeDescription: TSS is the sum of patient-reported scores (0-5 scale, 0=none, 1=very mild, 2=mild, 3=moderate, 4=severe, and 5=very severe) for each of 10 symptoms, specifically: (i) fatigue/weakness; (ii) pain, e.g., musculoskeletal, allodynia; (iii) brain fog; (iv) dysautonomia, e.g., tachycardia, hypotension, orthostatic intolerance, dizziness, vertigo, light-headedness, syncope, gastrointestinal/urinary issues; (v) headaches, including head/neck discomfort; (vi) sensory problems, e.g., sensitivity to light, sound, odor, taste, touch, or problems such as blurry vision, tinnitus, etc; (vii) sleep difficulties; (viii) shortness of breath; (ix) flu-like symptoms, e.g., sore throat, tender lymph nodes, feverishness, sinus or nasal congestion; and (x) mood disorders, e.g., anxiety, depression.

gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.title: Long-term Effects of Transcutaneous Vagal Nerve Stimulation on Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS)
gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.outcomeDescription: Autonomic symptoms will be assessed by the Composite Autonomic Symptom Scale (COMPASS 31) questionnaire. This is based on 31 items and a score ranging from 0 to100, 0 being the absence of symptom and 100 the greatest symptom intensity. The COMPASS 31 will be used to quantify the following autonomic symptoms: orthostatic intolerance, vasomotor, secretomotor, gastrointestinal, urinary and pupillomotor dysfunction symptoms.

gulf-war-illness:depleted-uranium-urinar.outcomeDescription: Urine was collected for 24hr during treatment. gulf-war-illness:heart-rate-variability-.outcomeMeasure: Improvement in symptoms of autonomic dysfunction as measured by the Composite Autonomic Symptom Score (COMPASS-31)
gulf-war-illness:heart-rate-variability-.outcomeDescription: The Composite Autonomic Symptom Score (COMPASS-31) is a refined, internally consistent, and markedly abbreviated quantitative measure of autonomic symptoms. It is based on the original Autonomic Symptom Profile (ASP) and COMPASS, applies a much-simplified scoring algorithm, and is suitable for widespread use in autonomic research and practice. It evaluates six domains of autonomic function: orthostatic intolerance, vasomotor, secretomotor, gastrointestinal, bladder, and pupillomotor domains. The score ranges from 0 (no symptoms) to 100 (strong autonomic dysfunction). Intra-patient change in autonomic dysfunction from baseline to week ten will be documented as indexed by the COMPASS-31.

It evaluates six domains of autonomic function: orthostatic intolerance, vasomotor, secretomotor, gastrointestinal, bladder, and pupillomotor domains. The score ranges from 0 (no symptoms) to 100 (strong autonomic dysfunction). Intra-patient change in autonomic dysfunction from baseline to follow-up points will be documented as indexed by the COMPASS-31.

gulf-war-illness:orthostatic-heart-rate-.title: Ensitrelvir for Viral Persistence and Inflammation in People Experiencing Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:orthostatic-heart-rate-.outcomeDescription: The active standing test is a non-invasive tool to assess orthostatic hypotension (OH) and postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS). In short, blood pressure and heart rate measurements were obtained after 5 minutes of resting supine and 1, 3, 5, and 10 minutes of continuous standing. A positive active stand test is defined as: those with a decline \>20 mmHg in systolic or \> 10 mmHg in diastolic blood pressure in at least two consecutive measurements, or those with an increase in heart rate \> 30 bpm on two consecutive measurements. Here we evaluate positive active stand tests as a binary outcome (yes, no), comparing the difference in proportion of participants with a positive active stand test at baseline and day 10 between the group treated with Ensitrelvir versus placebo.

gulf-war-illness:orthostatic-heart-rate-.title: RECOVER-VITAL: Platform Protocol, Appendix to Measure the Effects of Paxlovid on Long COVID Symptoms
gulf-war-illness:orthostatic-heart-rate-.outcomeMeasure: Percentage of Participants Who Improved in Autonomic Dysfunction Symptom Cluster, as Measured by the Active Stand Test
gulf-war-illness:orthostatic-heart-rate-.outcomeDescription: The active stand test is performed to assess presence of orthostatic intolerance, orthostatic hypotension, and postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome. The participant's blood pressure and heart rate are recorded after 5 minutes of lying and then at minutes 1, 3, 5, and 10 after standing. Changes in systolic blood pressure (SBP), diastolic blood pressure (DBP), and heart rate beats per minute (HR BPM) are assessed from lying to standing for 10 minutes. The major secondary endpoint for the autonomic dysfunction cluster is a binary endpoint defined as improvement in change from lying to standing in at least one of HR, DBP, or DBP from baseline to Day 90 (defined as an increase of at least 10mmHg in SBP, an increase of at least 5mmHg on DBP, or a decrease of at least 10 BPM on HR) and no worsening in any of HR, DBP, or DBP from baseline to Day 90 (defined as any decrease in SBP or DBP, or any increase in HR.

gulf-war-illness:cortisol-diurnal.outcomeDescription: Hair sample will be collected from the posterior vertex or nape area, where growth is continuous and less exposed to contaminants. Using clean scissors, the hair will be cut as close as possible to the scalp, obtaining a segment of approximately 3 cm in length. This will allow for the evaluation of cortisol levels accumulated over the last three months, as hair grows on average 1 cm per month.
