gulf-war-illness:brain-derived-neurotrop.outcomeDescription: Blood will be drawn from the antecubital vein into EDTA tubes, placed on ice, centrifuged, and plasma stored at -80°C until analysis. Brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) will be quantified as a brain plasticity marker.

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Brain Stimulation in Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Neuroinflammation and neuronal injury will be measured using MRS (metabolites for inflammation include choline, creatine and myo-inositol, and for neuronal injury N-acetyl-aspartate, glutamate and lactate).

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Tele-Rehabilitative Exercise Training Program in Nurses With Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: The point during exercise at which lactate (a byproduct of anaerobic metabolism) starts to accumulate in the blood faster than it can be removed. This indicates a shift from primarily aerobic energy production to more anaerobic energy production.

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Effects os Physiotherapeutic Rehabilitation on Function Autonomic and Inflammatory in Patients With Long Covid
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: the cardiac and inflammatory biomarkers, creatine phosphokinase isoenzyme MB (CKMB) associated with lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) and ferritin can be analysed.

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Effect of Metabolic Modulation on a Post-acute COVID-19 Vaccination Syndrome (PACVS) Cohort
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Blood lactate when the patient is at rest is taken using the Arkray Lactate Pro 2 Sports Blood Lactate Meter, which uses disposable test strips.

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: The Effect of Smart Sensor Combined With APP for Individualized Precise Exercise Training in Long Covid-19
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Anaerobic Threshold (AT) refers to the exercise intensity at which lactate begins to accumulate in the blood at a faster rate than it can be removed. It represents a transition point between predominantly aerobic metabolism (using oxygen) and increased anaerobic metabolism (without sufficient oxygen).

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: BIO 300 Oral Suspension in Previously Hospitalized Long COVID Patients
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: Change in Clinical Laboratory Values for Serum Enzymes
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Monitoring of blood serum levels for alkaline phosphatase (ALP), alanine transaminase (ALT), aspartate aminotransferase (AST) and lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) (all reported as Units/L)

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Treatment of Long COVID Symptoms Utilizing Autologous Stem Cells Following COVID-19 Infection
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: Assessment of Change in Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) Laboratory Testing Results
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) test results are to be assessed in this study are complete blood count with differential (CBC with diff), to identify any significant change in results positive or negative with the change reported as a percentage change from baseline

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Efficacy, Safety, Tolerability of AXA1125 in Fatigue After COVID-19 Infection
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: Change from baseline in serum lactate level after a 6-minute walk test

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Assessment of N-Acetylcysteine as Therapy for Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: Change of levels of ventricular CSF lactate of treatment response
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Levels of ventricular CSF lactate, as measured in vivo with 1H MRS

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: The Effect of Acupuncture Therapy on Cognitive Function in Post-COVID-19 Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: The change in level of lactate (Lac) at each hippocampus from baseline to the end of 8 weeks.
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: The level of Lac will be represented as the Lac to total creatine (Cr) ratio which is measured by Proton Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy(MRS)

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Efficacy and Safety of Fermented Velvet Antler Extract on Fatigue Recovery After Exercise
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Lactate was measured in study visit 1(0 week) and visit 3(12 week).

gulf-war-illness:lactate.title: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome: A Presumptive Mitochondrial Disorder
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeMeasure: Change in brain lactate and glutathione levels
gulf-war-illness:lactate.outcomeDescription: Patients will undergo nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy of the brain prior to starting therapy (baseline) and repeat it after 6 months of therapy.

gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.title: Acupuncture and Chinese Herbal Medicine for Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeMeasure: Change in Laboratory Biomarkers - Vitamin D levels.
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeDescription: Laboratory values will be compared from baseline to Week 8 to assess physiological effects of the intervention. Tests include vitamin D levels. Changes in these markers will indicate biological trends in inflammation and metabolic function.

gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.title: The Roles of Vitamin D and Microbiome in Children With Post-acute COVID-19 Syndromes (PACS) and Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeDescription: Vitamin D will be measured in a blood sample by ELISA to determine baseline status.

gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.title: Pilot Study of Vitamin K2 (MK-7) and Vitamin D3 Supplementation and the Effects on PASC Symptomatology and Inflammatory Biomarkers
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeMeasure: Change in Vitamin D3 levels as measured by blood test

gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.title: Efficacy of a Dietary Supplementation in Reducing Hospital Admissions for COVID-19. Randomized Clinical Trial
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeMeasure: Micronutrient basal status (Vitamin B1, Vitamin B6, 25-OH-Vitamin D and Folic Acid
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeDescription: Evaluation of micronutrient status prior to the nutritional supplement administration (in ng/mL)

gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.title: Magnesium and Vitamin D Combination for Post-COVID Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeMeasure: Change from Baseline Serum vitamin D levels at 4 months
gulf-war-illness:vitamin-d-25-oh.outcomeDescription:
Recovery of serum vitamin D levels from deficiency (\< 30 ng/mL) to normally (30 - 100 ng/mL).

The serum concentration of the 25 OH vitamin D fraction will be determined by the enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) method, the serum levels of magnesium and calcium by colorimetric techniques (A15 Clinical Analyzer, Biosystems, USA).

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Investigating the Effects of Intermittent Hypoxia-Hyperoxia Treatment (IHHT) in People With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS) to Improve Fatigue, Pain, and Quality of Life by Targeting Mitochondrial Dysfunction and Autonomic Nervous System Impairment
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Assessment of functional capacity using the FUNCAP-27 questionnaire.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Evaluating the Impact of a Functional and Cognitive Strategy in Patients With Long Covid-19
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Composite Change in Functional Capacity, Cognitive Function, Health-Related Quality of Life, and Strength
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription:
The primary outcome is a composite measure evaluating the aggregated change across four key functional and cognitive scales:

* Functional Capacity with six-minute walk test.
* Cognitive Function with Montreal Cognitive Assessment, MoCA. The minimum score is 0 points, which would indicate very severe cognitive impairment, since the maximum total score is 30 points. Normally, a score of 26 or higher is considered within the normal range, although the range may vary depending on factors such as the patient's age and educational level. A score below 26 could suggest possible cognitive impairment, and the lower the score, the more indicative it may be of severe cognitive problems.
* Health-Related Quality of Life with EuroQol 5 Dimensions 5 Levels, EQ-5D-5L. Maximum value: 1 This value corresponds to the best possible health state. Minimum value: -0.594 This value corresponds to the worst possible.
* Handgrip Strength with hydraulic dynamometer.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Long Covid (LC)-REVITALIZE - A Long Covid Repurposed Drug Study
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: General participant reported severity of post-exertional malaise (PEM) using the FUNCAP27 questionnaire
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: To compare symptom burden of participants with Long Covid treated with study drug versus placebo by measuring the change in scores of the Functional Capacity (FUNCAP)27 questionnaire from baseline to the interim and final analyses. The FUNCAP27 questionnaire consists of 27 items related to assessing functional capacity and the consequences of performing activities. Each item is scored on a scale of 0-6, with 0 indicating the participant is unable to complete the activity and 6 indicating the activity is unproblematic and does not affect other activities.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Plitidepsin in Adults With Post-COVID-19 Condition (PCC)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: To compare functional capacity changes in patients from each group using the post-COVID-19 Functional State (PCFS) scale.
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Difference between groups in functional capacity on the PCFS scale on day 10 (±2), day 30 (±2), and day 90 (±5) of the follow-up period.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Ensitrelvir for Viral Persistence and Inflammation in People Experiencing Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with Ensitrelvir versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean DASI at Day 10 post-randomization. The Duke Activity Status Index is a patient-reported estimate of functional capacity, maximal oxygen consumption (VO2 max) and maximum metabolic equivalent of tasks (METs). The DASI questionnaire produces a score between 0 and 58.2 points, which is linearly correlated with a patient's VO2 max and METs, as measured from cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPET). It inquires about a person's ability to perform self-care, walk, climb stairs, run, do house and yard work, engage in sexual intercourse, and perform moderate recreational activities. A higher score indicates higher functional capacity.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Is Swimming a More Tolerable Form of Movement for Individuals With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/ Chronic Fatigue Syndrome?
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The functional capacity 27 scale will be used to assess the functional severity of each participant's condition. The scale has 27 statements, that are ranked from on a likert scale of 0-6, with 0 being "unable to perform that activity", and 6 being "unproblematic - does not affect other activities." The average score from all 27 statements is used as the overall score. Only participants with a score ≥3.0 and \<5.8 will be included in the study (representing mild-to-moderate ME/CFS).

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Diaphragmatic Breathing Exercises for Post-COVID-19 Diaphragmatic Dysfunction (DD)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: It is a simple test that requires no specialized equipment or advanced training for physicians and assesses the submaximal level of functional capacity of an individual while walking on a flat, hard surface in a period of 6 min. It is used to assess the response to a medical therapy, and in cardiac/pulmonary rehabilitation.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Vagal Approaches on Long COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Monitoring steps will allow us to assess whether SAT and Truvaga Electrical Vagus Nerve Stimulator are associated with an increase in average daily steps, which may indicate enhanced functional capacity or reduced fatigue.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Whole-Body Electrostimulation for Functional Recovery in Post-COVID Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Functional capacity will be evaluated using the Six-Minute Walk Test (6MWT), which measures the maximum distance walked in six minutes as an indicator of aerobic capacity and functional exercise tolerance.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: The Long COVID Treatment Trial
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Secondary Outcome: Changes in functional capacity as measured by Functional Capacity (FUNCAP) survey
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The 27-question version of FUNCAP has a minimum score of 0 and a maximum score of 162, with a lower score indicating lower functional capacity.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Physiology of Long COVID-19 and the Impact of Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation on Quality-of-Life and Functional Capacity
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Characterizing the impact of exercise training (cardiac rehabilitation) on functional capacity among patients with Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: functional capacity as determined by VO2max

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Anti-SARS-CoV-2 Monoclonal Antibodies for Long COVID (COVID-19)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This measure will evaluate whether there is a difference between treatment with AER002 versus placebo in baseline adjusted mean DASI at Day 90 post-infusion. The Duke Activity Status Index is a patient-reported estimate of functional capacity, maximal oxygen consumption (VO2 max) and maximum metabolic equivalent of tasks (METs). The DASI questionnaire produces a score between 0 and 58.2 points, which is linearly correlated with a patient's VO2 max and METs, as measured from cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPET). It inquires about a person's ability to perform self-care, walk, climb stairs, run, do house and yard work, engage in sexual intercourse, and perform moderate recreational activities. A higher score indicates higher functional capacity.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Heat thErapy And mobiLity in COVID-19 Survivors
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Test of physical functional capacity

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: LIFT: Life Improvement Trial
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription:
Change in the total FUNCAP55 questionnaire score

The FUNCAP55 is a questionnaire developed to assess the functional capacity of patients. Each question is answered on a 6-point scale. The greater the score, the better the functioning.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Use of a Cysteine-rich Whey Protein Isolate in Post COVID-19 Cognitive Impairment
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Functional capacity will be measured by the 30-second sit-to-stand test. The number of repetitions completed will be compared between baseline and study completion.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: SARS-CoV-2 Specific Monoclonal Antibody for Post-COVID-19 Conditions (Long COVID)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Change in Heart Rate post 6-minute walk distance (6MWT), in meters, from baseline to Weeks 12 and 24.Unit: Beats per minute (bpm)

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Cognitive Rehabilitation Therapy for COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Change in functional capacity performance z score
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Change in composite z score

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Cardiopulmonary Rehabilitation in Post-acute COVID-19 Syndrome
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: CPET is a non-invasive examination, which provides assessment of the integrative exercise responses involving the pulmonary, cardiovascular, hematopoietic, neuropsychological, and skeletal muscle systems. It permits the evaluation of both submaximal and peak exercise responses. CPET is currently act as a clinical applications for the evaluation of undiagnosed exercise intolerance and for the objective determination of functional capacity and impairment.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: CISCO-21 Prevent and Treat Long COVID-19.
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: This is a validated measure of functional capacity, with test-retest reliability and evidence of being responsive to rehabilitation interventions.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Pulmonary Rehabilitation for Long COVID (Post COVID-19 Condition)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Predictors of response in 6- minute walk distance (6MWD)
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: The predictors will be based on baseline symptom scores (CAT, mMRC, CIS), baseline 6MWD and hospitalisation status.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Mindfulness in Post Acute Sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 Infection (PASC) Dysautonomia
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Change from baseline of Six Minute Walk in hemodynamic parameters, exercise tolerance
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Six Minute Walk. The six-minute walk (6MWT) was developed in 1963 by Balke to evaluate functional capacity and endurance during physical activity. The standardized six-minute walk test (6MWT) measures the maximum distance an individual is able to walk over a total of six minutes. The individual is allowed to self-pace and rest as needed as they traverse back and forth along a flat marked walkway. Baseline heart rate and oxygen saturation are measured then continuously monitored to identify the lowest oxygen saturation, which may occur before the end of the test. The patient's baseline and post-test perceived dyspnea and fatigue are rated using the Borg scale 0-10 (0 none- 10 maximum).

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Effects of Physiotherapy Via Telerehabilitation in Patients With COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: using Duke Activity status index The final score ranges between zero and 58.2 points; The higher the score, the better the functional capacity.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: tDCS in the Management of Post-COVID Disorders
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: the participant's functional capacity and aerobic resistance will be evaluated through the 6-minute walk test. The test measures the distance that the volunteer covers in a period of 6 minutes walking at a steady speed.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: The Use of Photobiomodulation in the Treatment of Oral Complaints of Long COVID-19.A Randomized Controlled Trial.
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeMeasure: Brazilian version of the SF 36 Quality of Life Scale
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Assessment of general quality of life, translated and validated for the Brazilian population, composed of assessments in the following domains: functional capacity, limitation due to physical aspects, pain, general health status, vitality, social aspects, emotional aspects and mental health.

gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.title: Telerehabilitation for Post COVID-19 Condition
gulf-war-illness:6-minute-walk-distance.outcomeDescription: Strength, assistance with walking, rising from a chair, climbing stairs, and falls questionnaire (SARC-F)

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: PERCEIVE-Outreach: A Scalable, Risk-Based Model for Managing Persistent Cardiovascular Impact in Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in Assessment of Quality of Life (AQoL) 8D at 12 & 24 months
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Publicly accessible Australian utility score (online); range 0.06 (worse) to 1.00 (better)

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: sElective Serotonin reuPtake inhibitoRs In posT-covid After COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Health-related Quality of Life
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Dutch-Flemish Patient-Reported Outcome Measurement Information System (PROMIS) Profile-29. Higher scores indicate better outcome.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of PNMR as Treatment for Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in QoL as measured by the Medical Outcomes Study Short Form 36 (SF-36) at 3 and 6 weeks on treatment.
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The SF-36 (Short Form 36) is a self-reported survey that measures 36 individual health-related indicators of Quality of Life (QOL). It asks the patient to rate their: General Health; General Health now compared to one week ago; along with more detailed evaluations of Physical Health, Role Limitations (Physical), Emotional Health, Role Limitations (Emotional), Social Functioning; and to rate other factors related to Pain, Energy, and Mood as compared to the week prior. Each question has a unique set of answers specific to the question. For some questions a higher score means a worse outcome, whereas for other questions a higher score means a better outcome. This method requires that the patient think through each answer individually.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Diaphragmatic Breathing Exercises With KOTS Currents in Patients With Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Quality of Life with SF-36 questionnaire
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Quality of life will be assessed using the SF-36 questionnaire. The outcome will evaluate the change in quality-of-life dimensions from baseline to after the three-week intervention. Higher scores indicate better quality of life.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: RECOVER-AUTONOMIC: Platform Protocol, Appendix A (IVIG)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS-29) + 2 Questionnaire
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription:
The PROMIS-29 consists of 29 items that assess general domains of health and functioning, including overall physical health, mental health, social health, pain, fatigue, and overall perceived quality of life.

The PROMIS-29+2 is used to calculate a preference score (PROPr) by the addition of two Cognitive Function Ability items. Scores will be reported as T scores ranging from 0 to 100, with a score of 60 being 1 standard deviation above the mean. Higher scores indicate worse overall health.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Evaluating the Effects of a Fermented Diet on Microbiome Diversity in Individuals With Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Assessment of quality of life
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Measured using the self-reported EuroQol-5D-3L questionnaire. Total questions: 6. Mobility: 1, Self-Care: 1, Usual Activities: 1, Pain/discomfort: 1, Anxiety/depression: 1, Health State - Visual Analog Scale: 1. 3L - 3 levels of severity: no problems, some problems, extreme problems. The visual analog scale ranges from 0 to 100 with higher scores reflecting better perceived current health-related quality of life state.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: NE3107 in Adults With Neurological Symptoms of Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change from Baseline in SF-12 Health Survey (Physical and Mental Component Scores)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Generic quality-of-life assessment evaluating physical (PCS) and mental (MCS) health domains

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Diagnostic and Clinical Management of Non-Obstructive Myocardial Ischemia in Patients With Long Covid-19 Syndrome and New Onset Chest Pain
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: The changes in angina symptom and quality of life assessed by Seattle Questionnaire of Angina score in the treatment guided group comparing to the standard of care group
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The Seattle Angina Questionnaire (SAQ) measures 5 dimensions of coronary artery disease (physical limit, stability, frequency, satisfaction, disease perception) on a 0-100 scale, where higher scores indicate better health status. Scores are interpreted as: 0-24 (Poor), 25-49 (Fair), 50-74 (Good), 75-100 (Excellent).

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Taurine Supplementation in Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Health Related Quality of Life - SF-36 (v.1)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Measurement of change in quality of life using the Quality of Life - SF-36 (v.1) assessment tool.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: AT1001 for the Treatment of Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Adverse Event Profiling and Time to Symptom Resolution
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The primary objective of this study is to evaluate the safety and efficacy of Larazotide (AT1001) versus placebo in children and young adults 7 to ≤50 years of age who present with symptoms of Long COVID. Safety will be assessed by means of adverse event monitoring, and efficacy will be evaluated using the Symptom Burden Questionnaire™ for Long COVID (SBQ™-LC) survey. Additional surveys will be used to monitor symptoms, quality of life and limitation of activities before, during and after treatment with Larazotide or Placebo.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Investigating the Effects of Intermittent Hypoxia-Hyperoxia Treatment (IHHT) in People With Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS) to Improve Fatigue, Pain, and Quality of Life by Targeting Mitochondrial Dysfunction and Autonomic Nervous System Impairment
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in SF-36 Vitality Domain score from baseline to post-treatment
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Change in the vitality domain of the 36-Item Short Form Health Survey (SF-36), assessing fatigue and energy-related quality of life.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: SARS-CoV-2 and Herpesvirus Inhibition for Ending Long COVID Dysfunction
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: The EuroQol Five-Dimensional Health Questionnaire (EQ-5D-5L)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The EQ-5D-5L is a validated, standardized, generic instrument that is a preference-based health- related quality of life questionnaire. The descriptive system comprises five dimensions: mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort and anxiety/depression. Each dimension has 5 levels: no problems, slight problems, moderate problems, severe problems and extreme problems. The patient is asked to indicate their health state by ticking the box next to the most appropriate statement in each of the five dimensions. This decision results in a 1-digit number that expresses the level selected for that dimension. The digits for the five dimensions can be combined into a 5-digit number that describes the patient's health state. Full scale from 5 to 25, with higher score indicating poorer health outcomes.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Fatigability in Long COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Health-related quality of life (SF-36)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The 36-item short-form (SF-36) is a multi-item scale that assesses eight health concepts: 1) limitations in physical activities because of health problems; 2) limitations in social activities because of physical or emotional problems; 3) limitations in usual role activities because of physical health problems; 4) bodily pain; 5) general mental health (psychological distress and well-being); 6) limitations in usual role activities because of emotional problems; 7) vitality (energy and fatigue); and 8) general health perceptions.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Low Dose Sirolimus in People With Post-Acute Sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC) Long COVID-19
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: European Quality of Life-Visual Analogue Scale (EQ-VAS)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The EQ VAS records the patient's self-rated health on a visual analogue scale where the endpoints are labelled 'The best health you can imagine' (100) and 'The worst health you can imagine' (0), with higher scores indicating better health state. The VAS can be used as a quantitative measure of health outcome that reflects the patient's own judgement.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: A Study to Assess Anktiva in Patients With Long Covid-19.
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in other assessments (eg, EuroQoL Quality of Life) from baseline, intervention 2, FU2.3 (2 weeks after last NAI administration), FU2.5, and EOS.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Trial EValuating Baricitinib on PERSistent NEurologic and Cardiopulmonary Symptoms of Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Quality-of-life measures including the EuroQOL-5D-5L at 3-, 6-, and 12-months
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The 5-Level EuroQol-5D health questionnaire (EQ-5D-5L) is a standardized instrument for use as a measure of health outcome that includes a descriptive system consisting of 5 dimensions (mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort, and anxiety/depression). The index value total (which is country-specific) ranges from a minimum value of -1 to a maximum value of +1. A higher EQ-5D-5L index value represents better quality of life (QoL), thus a positive change in the index value represents improved QoL.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: RCT of Mind-body in Long COVID and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Physical functioning
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Physical functioning of health-related quality of life (HRQL) as measured by the Short Form 36-item Health Survey (SF-36) (V1)

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: RECOVER-ENERGIZE Platform Protocol_Appendix B (Structured Pacing (PEM))
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in quality of life, as measured by the PROMIS-29+2
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The PROMIS-29+2 is used to calculate a preference score (PROPr) by the addition of two Cognitive Function Ability items. Preference-based scores provide an overall summary of health-related quality of life on a common metric. Preference-based scores summarize multiple domains on a metric ranging from 0 (as bad as dead) to 1 (perfect or ideal health).

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation as a Complementary Therapy to Exercise in Chronic Fatigue
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Medical Outcome Study Short Form questionnaire (MOS-SF 12)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Quality of life assessed by the MOS-SF 12 questionnaire , score from 0 to 100. A low score reflects a perception of poor health, loss of function and the presence of pain. A high score reflects a perception of good health, absence of functional deficit and pain.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Cognitive Muscular Therapy for Patients With Long-COVID and Breathing Pattern Disorder
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Change in EQ-5D-5L
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Used to capture an individual's quality of life through their ability to complete daily living activities. Each heading is rated from no problems through to unable to complete the task.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Digital Multimodal Rehabilitation for People With Post-acute COVID-19 Syndrome.
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Differences between groups in scores of Quality of Life
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Quality of Life is measured with EuroQol a self-completion questionnaire, which consists of five questions: covering mobility, hygiene, activities, pain, and anxiety. The descriptive system divides each of the 5 dimensions into three levels of response: the absence of a problem, some problem, and extreme problem. Lower scores indicate better outcomes. In addition, the questionnaire has a plus scale where the participants rated their health state on a scale of 0-100. In this scale, higher scores indicate better outcome.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Pycnogenol® in Post-COVID-19 Condition
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Health-related quality of life (EQ-5D-5L)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: The EQ-5D-5L assesses the 5 dimensions mobility, self-care, usual activities, pain/discomfort, anxiety/depression. The EQ-5D-5L scores each dimension on five levels of severity ranging from 1 = "no problems" to 5 = "extreme problems. The instrument will be used at baseline and after 12 weeks.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Tele-exercise Training Program for Long COVID
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Health-Related Quality of Life
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription:
The brief World Health Organization Quality of Life questionnaire includes 26 standardized items covering four domains: physical, psychological, social, and environmental. It also contains two general items assessing overall quality of life and general health. The Taiwanese version adds two culturally specific items related to social respect/acceptance and eating/food.

All items are rated on a 5-point Likert scale, where higher scores reflect better quality of life. Scores for each domain are calculated by averaging the item scores within that domain and then multiplying by 4, yielding a final score range from 4 to 20.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Evaluating the Impact of a Functional and Cognitive Strategy in Patients With Long Covid-19
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: Composite Change in Functional Capacity, Cognitive Function, Health-Related Quality of Life, and Strength
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription:
The primary outcome is a composite measure evaluating the aggregated change across four key functional and cognitive scales:

* Functional Capacity with six-minute walk test.
* Cognitive Function with Montreal Cognitive Assessment, MoCA. The minimum score is 0 points, which would indicate very severe cognitive impairment, since the maximum total score is 30 points. Normally, a score of 26 or higher is considered within the normal range, although the range may vary depending on factors such as the patient's age and educational level. A score below 26 could suggest possible cognitive impairment, and the lower the score, the more indicative it may be of severe cognitive problems.
* Health-Related Quality of Life with EuroQol 5 Dimensions 5 Levels, EQ-5D-5L. Maximum value: 1 This value corresponds to the best possible health state. Minimum value: -0.594 This value corresponds to the worst possible.
* Handgrip Strength with hydraulic dynamometer.

gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.title: Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Plitidepsin in Adults With Post-COVID-19 Condition (PCC)
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeMeasure: To compare changes in terms of Quality of Life (QoL) for each group.
gulf-war-illness:sf-36-physical-componen.outcomeDescription: Difference between groups according to the EuroQoL-5D questionnaire on day 10 (±2), day 30 (±2), and day 90 (±5) of the follow-up period.
